subject: Social Science (CBSE - Social and Political Life– II)

activities_projects.title: Equality Mind Map
steps: Draw a central circle with the word “Equality”
Branch out struggles such as caste discrimination, women’s rights, and child labour
Write one key fact under each branch
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand different movements for equality

activities_projects.title: Role Play: Social Reformers
steps: Select a social reformer discussed in the chapter
Prepare 2–3 lines about their contribution
Perform a short role play for the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about struggles against discrimination
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of content and confidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Timeline of Equality Movements
steps: Draw a horizontal line
Mark important equality events with dates
Write brief descriptions under each date
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand when major movements happened

activities_projects.title: Interview a Community Member
steps: Prepare 5 questions about equality and fairness
Interview a family or community member
Write a summary of their views
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect chapter ideas with real life
activities_projects.evaluation: Question relevance and summary quality (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poster on Equality
steps: Think of a slogan for equality
Create a poster showing people united
Decorate with symbols like scales or hands

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion: What is Fair?
steps: In groups, discuss a situation where fairness is needed
Note different viewpoints
Share conclusions with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn multiple perspectives on equality

activities_projects.title: Success Stories of Equality
steps: Research how school or law helped improve equality
List 3 success stories
Write why they are inspiring
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize progress made in society

activities_projects.title: Equality Vocabulary Match
steps: List important words like ‘discrimination’, ‘justice’, ‘rights’
Write definitions and match them
Make flashcards

activities_projects.title: Write a Diary Entry
activities_projects.objective: Empathise with people who fought for equality
steps: Imagine you are a child in a society facing discrimination
Write a diary about your day
Include your hopes for fairness and equality
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand emotional impact of inequality

activities_projects.title: Mini Quiz on Struggles for Equality
steps: Prepare 10 questions based on the chapter
Ask classmates to answer them
Keep score for correct answers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students review chapter through peer learning
